drive.name: ShiftWork Intake Answer - Northstar Renovations - Marketing Visual QA.json

handoffPayload.name: Mara Ellison
handoffPayload.agentTask: Landing page visual quality monitoring and email alerts
handoffPayload.buildRequest: Build a visual quality agent that screenshots our marketing landing page on desktop and mobile, catches visual inconsistencies that hurt conversion, and drafts an email alert when something breaks.

researchBrief.business: Home renovation contractor using a public landing page and reviews to convert homeowners into estimate requests.
researchBrief.likelyCoreProblem: Marketing edits can hide the hero image, review proof, offer, or quote CTA on mobile before the owner notices.
assumedOrUnverified: Actual ad landing URL should be confirmed.
Baseline screenshots are simulated until OAuth Drive or browser capture is wired.

selectedQuestionnaire.reason: The target protects the public marketing funnel: audience message, content proof, offer, reviews, and referral/conversion CTA.

answers.answer: Sound warm, expert, and trustworthy. The page should feel like a local renovation specialist, not a national franchise.
Reach homeowners within 30 miles who want kitchen, bath, or basement updates and need a quote without calling first.
Google reviews and before/after photos matter most. If those sections disappear or overlap, leads trust us less.
Check https://northstarrenovations.com/ at desktop 1280x900 and mobile 390x844. The hero image, headline, offer, reviews, before/after image, and Get a Quote CTA must all be visible.
The company likely uses Google Business reviews, but the review source is unconfirmed.

checks: Get a Quote CTA visible without horizontal scroll

emailAlerts.sendOnlyWhen: One or more inconsistency criteria are detected.

acceptanceInputs: seeded hidden Get a Quote CTA
visualQualityAgentRequest.mustPassInput: mobile homepage screenshot with hero, reviews, offer, and Get a Quote CTA visible

expectedAgent.name: Marketing Landing Page Visual QA Agent

drive.name: ShiftWork Intake Answer - Northstar Renovations - Sales Visual QA.json

handoffPayload.name: Mara Ellison
handoffPayload.agentTask: Quote and booking page visual quality monitoring
handoffPayload.buildRequest: Build a visual QA agent that checks our quote form and booking page screenshots, then drafts an alert if a lead cannot request an estimate.

researchBrief.business: Home renovation contractor where booked estimates and quote requests drive revenue.
researchBrief.likelyCoreProblem: A broken quote form, hidden phone number, or unusable booking CTA can silently drop sales leads.
assumedOrUnverified: Booking tool and quote form provider need owner confirmation.

selectedQuestionnaire.reason: The target protects lead follow-up, quotes, estimates, and appointment booking.

answers.answer: A web lead should get to the quote form or booking CTA immediately. What actually happens is sometimes the button moves below the fold or the form is cut off on phones.
New web leads should be able to request an estimate immediately, especially on mobile.
Prospects book through the website form first. The agent should check that the form, phone number, and Book Estimate CTA are visible.
Check /quote and /book if present; otherwise check the homepage quote form section. Desktop 1280x900 and mobile 390x844 are required.
Calendly may be used for booking, but the scheduling tool is unconfirmed.

emailAlerts.sendOnlyWhen: A quote or booking path is visually broken.

visualQualityAgentRequest.mustPassInput: mobile quote form screenshot with form fields and CTA visible

expectedAgent.name: Sales Quote Page Visual QA Agent

drive.name: ShiftWork Intake Answer - Northstar Renovations - Fulfillment Visual QA.json

handoffPayload.name: Mara Ellison
handoffPayload.agentTask: Customer welcome and job-status page visual monitoring
handoffPayload.buildRequest: Build a visual QA agent that screenshots our customer welcome/status page and drafts an email if the project timeline, checklist, or document links are visually broken.

researchBrief.business: Renovation contractor coordinating customers after estimate acceptance.
researchBrief.likelyCoreProblem: Customers lose trust when welcome steps, project timelines, or document upload links are visually broken.
assumedOrUnverified: Customer portal URL is simulated and must be confirmed.

selectedQuestionnaire.reason: The target protects new-customer welcome, job updates, job tracking, and paperwork flows.

answers.answer: After a deal closes, the customer should see what happens next, what we need from them, and when the job starts.
Customers should not have to chase us for status. The project timeline and next step should be readable on the status page.
Every job needs contract, deposit invoice, selections checklist, and project update documents to be reachable.
Check the customer welcome/status page at desktop 1280x900 and mobile 390x844. Timeline, checklist, upload link, and document CTA must render.
The portal may be in Jobber or a custom page, but the system of record is unconfirmed.

emailAlerts.sendOnlyWhen: A customer handoff or project-update visual path is broken.

visualQualityAgentRequest.mustPassInput: customer welcome page screenshot with checklist, timeline, and document link visible

expectedAgent.name: Fulfillment Status Page Visual QA Agent

drive.name: ShiftWork Intake Answer - Northstar Renovations - Service Visual QA.json

handoffPayload.name: Mara Ellison
handoffPayload.agentTask: Support and contact page visual monitoring
handoffPayload.buildRequest: Build a visual QA agent that screenshots our contact and FAQ page and drafts an alert if customers cannot find phone, contact form, FAQ answers, or escalation instructions.

researchBrief.business: Renovation contractor receiving customer questions through phone, web form, and email.
researchBrief.likelyCoreProblem: Missed calls and repeat questions get worse if contact, FAQ, or escalation information is hidden on mobile.
assumedOrUnverified: Exact after-hours call handling process needs owner confirmation.

selectedQuestionnaire.reason: The target protects call answering, customer questions, reminders, tone, and escalation paths.

answers.answer: If we cannot pick up, customers should still see the phone number, contact form, and what to expect next.
Customers ask about estimate timing, project status, deposits, and what happens if weather delays the job.
Escalate complaints, urgent safety issues, and angry customers. The page should make that handoff clear.
Check /contact and /faq if present; otherwise check the contact section on the homepage. Desktop and mobile screenshots are required.
The company may use a website form plus phone, but live chat availability is unconfirmed.

emailAlerts.sendOnlyWhen: A contact, FAQ, or escalation visual path is broken.

visualQualityAgentRequest.mustPassInput: mobile contact screenshot with phone, form, FAQ, and escalation copy visible

expectedAgent.name: Service Contact Page Visual QA Agent

drive.name: ShiftWork Intake Answer - Northstar Renovations - Ops Visual QA.json

handoffPayload.name: Mara Ellison
handoffPayload.agentTask: Operations page visual quality monitoring and email alerts
handoffPayload.buildRequest: Build a visual QA agent that checks our operations/admin page screenshots for crew schedule, invoice status, inbox triage, and hiring blocks, then drafts an alert if something visually breaks.

researchBrief.business: Renovation contractor coordinating crews, invoices, inbox triage, and hiring from simple internal pages.
researchBrief.likelyCoreProblem: Back-office pages can visually break and hide crew schedule, invoice, inbox, or hiring tasks before the owner notices.
assumedOrUnverified: The real operations URL is internal and should be confirmed before live browser access.

selectedQuestionnaire.reason: The target protects admin, finance, crew scheduling, inbox management, and hiring operations.

answers.answer: Inbox noise buries quote requests, invoice questions, and crew schedule changes. The admin page should keep those blocks visible.
We schedule crews and jobs from a shared calendar and texts. The crew schedule block must not disappear or overlap.
The admin task that eats the most time is checking invoices, schedule changes, and hiring follow-ups across different places.
Check /ops or the internal admin landing page at desktop and mobile sizes. Crew schedule, invoice status, inbox triage, and hiring cards must all render.
QuickBooks, Google Calendar, and Gmail are likely tools, but connector choices are unconfirmed.

emailAlerts.sendOnlyWhen: An operations visual path is broken.

visualQualityAgentRequest.mustPassInput: ops page screenshot with crew schedule, invoice status, inbox triage, and hiring cards visible
